core_mechanism: An online 'power of choices' (Achlioptas) selection rule — at each step choose one of K independent uniformly-random candidate links to add to an incrementally grown random network — that shifts the location of the sharp critical threshold at which a global spanning structure first emerges, with the threshold's scaling set by the number of offered choices K
brief_reason: The abstract describes an initially empty network to which one of K uniformly-random candidate edges is added per round so as to reach a spanning (Hamilton) cycle as fast as possible, producing three distinct threshold regimes whose scaling depends on K — i.e., adaptive best-of-K choice tuning the location of an emergence/percolation-type threshold.
